title: Quiz: Ansatz Design
description: Test your understanding of variational circuit design principles

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of hardware-efficient ansatze over chemistry-inspired ansatze?

options.text: They always find better ground state energies
They minimize circuit depth and respect device connectivity

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansatze are designed to minimize circuit depth and use only native gates with connectivity matching the hardware. This reduces noise accumulation but may sacrifice physical constraints like particle number conservation.

questions.question: In the UCCSD ansatz, what does the cluster operator T = T1 + T2 represent?

options.text: Single and double excitation operators from occupied to virtual orbitals
Temperature parameters for thermal state preparation

questions.explanation: In UCCSD, T1 represents single excitations (one electron from occupied to virtual orbital) and T2 represents double excitations (two electrons). These operators are applied as e^(T-T^dagger) to the Hartree-Fock reference.

questions.question: What does high ansatz expressibility indicate?

options.text: The ansatz is easy to optimize
The ansatz can explore states close to Haar-random

questions.explanation: Expressibility measures how uniformly an ansatz can sample the Hilbert space. High expressibility means the distribution of states approaches the Haar-random distribution, indicating the ansatz can represent a wide variety of quantum states.

questions.question: What is a barren plateau in the context of variational quantum algorithms?

options.text: A region where the energy is exactly zero
A region where gradients vanish exponentially with system size
A local minimum in the energy landscape

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus are regions where the variance of gradients decreases exponentially with the number of qubits, making gradient-based optimization essentially impossible for large systems. This is a fundamental trainability challenge.

questions.question: ADAPT-VQE builds the ansatz iteratively by selecting operators that maximize the gradient magnitude at each step.
questions.explanation: True. ADAPT-VQE selects operators from a pool based on their gradient magnitude (|<psi|[H,A]|psi>|), choosing the operator that most effectively lowers the energy at each iteration. This creates a problem-tailored, compact ansatz.

questions.question: Which strategy helps mitigate barren plateaus in deep variational circuits?

options.text: Increasing the number of measurement shots
Layer-wise training starting from shallow circuits

questions.explanation: Layer-wise training starts with shallow circuits (which don't have barren plateaus) and gradually adds layers while maintaining good parameter initialization. Random initialization and global cost functions actually worsen barren plateaus.

questions.question: For molecular ground state calculations, why is UCCSD often preferred over hardware-efficient ansatze?

options.text: UCCSD preserves particle number and spin symmetries
UCCSD is immune to barren plateaus

questions.explanation: UCCSD preserves physical symmetries like particle number and spin that are crucial for chemistry problems. Hardware-efficient ansatze may explore unphysical states that violate these conservation laws, wasting optimization effort.

questions.question: Which factors contribute to barren plateaus? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Global cost functions measuring all qubits

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus arise from: (a) deep circuits that approach Haar-random distributions, (b) global cost functions where operators act on all qubits, and (d) noise that depolarizes states toward the maximally mixed state. Shallow circuits typically avoid barren plateaus.
